# kyt-entity-registry

> Open-source registry of 800+ cryptocurrency entity logos (exchanges,
> DEX, bridges, DeFi protocols, mixers, hacks, sanctioning bodies) —
> 160×160 RGBA PNG, served free via the jsDelivr CDN, with a fuzzy
> resolver for freeform labels and an MCP server for Claude agents.

This file follows the https://llmstxt.org/ convention. If you are an
LLM-powered agent fetching this, you can consume the registry via any
of the URLs below without browsing the full site.

## Quick facts

- **Repo**: https://github.com/maslovsa/kyt-entity-registry (MIT licensed)
- **Live site**: https://maslovsa.github.io/kyt-entity-registry/
- **CDN root**: `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/maslovsa/kyt-entity-registry@main`
- **Entity count**: ~800 (updated nightly from sdn_api label claims)
- **Logo shape**: 160×160 RGBA PNG, optimised, ≤ 50 KB each
- **Logo URL pattern**: `<CDN>/logos/<dir>/<slug>.png`
  where `<dir>` is the category (plural for `exchange → exchanges`,
  everything else 1:1) and `<slug>` is the entity's arkham_slug.

## Data artefacts (machine-readable)

- **entities.csv** — the canonical row list.
  `<CDN>/entities.csv`
  columns: entity_name, category_slug, importance, claim_count,
  max_trust, severity, networks, sources, arkham_slug,
  canonical_domain, logo_status, logo_updated_at, manual_lock,
  logo_hash
- **logos/_index.json** — flat `{ "<dir>/<slug>": true, … }` listing
  only entities with a REAL source hit (excludes placeholders). Good
  for "does a real logo exist?" checks.
  `<CDN>/logos/_index.json`
- **logos/_lookup.json** — fuzzy-match index with pre-extracted
  keywords per entity. Consumed by lookup.js, the MCP server, and
  any custom resolver.
  `<CDN>/logos/_lookup.json`

## Resolution strategies

### A — when you already have a canonical entity name

`(category_slug, entity_name)` → deterministic URL. See
`docs/CONSUMERS.md` for the TS + Python helpers (category→dir map,
name-to-slug normalization with suffix stripping).

### B — when you have a freeform label

Load `_lookup.json` once per session, then score keyword-overlap
between the tokenized label and each entry's `kw` array. Top score
wins, tie-broken by importance and real-vs-placeholder. See
`lookup.js` (vanilla ES module) for a reference implementation; ~50
lines total.

### C — via MCP (for Claude Code / Claude Desktop)

A stdio MCP server runs locally. Configure in `~/.claude/settings.json`:

```json
"mcpServers": {
  "kyt-entity-registry": {
    "command": "uvx",
    "args": ["--from",
             "git+https://github.com/maslovsa/kyt-entity-registry.git#subdirectory=mcp/python",
             "kyt-entity-registry-mcp"]
  }
}
```

Tools exposed:
- `resolve_logo(label, category?, prefer_real=true)` →
  `{url, slug, name, category, real, importance}` | null
- `list_categories()` → `[{slug, dir, count}]`
- `list_entities(category?, limit=50, offset=0)` → page of entries
- `get_entity(category, slug)` → full row
- `search_entities(query, category?, limit=10)` → top-N matches

TypeScript twin available at `mcp/ts/`.

## Categories (13 total)

`exchange` (→ `exchanges/`), `dex`, `bridge`, `defi`, `wallet`,
`mining`, `psp`, `bot`, `gambling`, `nft_marketplace`, `mixer`,
`hack`, `sanctioned`.

## Source chain (for enrichment)

When a logo is missing, the nightly cron tries in order:

1. Arkham Intelligence static bucket
2. Brandfetch CDN (keyed on canonical_domain)
3. DefiLlama icons (defi/dex/bridge only)
4. Favicon crawl of canonical_domain
5. Placeholder (`logos/404.png` bytes, category-specific for hack)

Each entry's `logo_status` column records which source won.

## How to correct a logo

- Flag + propose via the audit UI: https://maslovsa.github.io/kyt-entity-registry/audit.html
  The tool exports a rework CSV with embedded PNG data URLs.
- Or drop files on the landing page "Have better logos?" section for
  bulk upload — generates the same CSV format.
- Hand the CSV to a maintainer (PR against the repo or attach). The
  `scripts/rework_from_report.py` applier walks the CSV and lands
  suggestions as manual overrides with `manual_lock=true`.

## Related docs

- `docs/CONSUMERS.md` — TS + Python helpers, fuzzy resolver recipe,
  SHA-pinning for compliance PDFs, cache TTL notes.
- `docs/PROVIDERS.md` — contract for projects that ship fresh CSV
  exports (today: sdn_api); column ownership + never-do list.
- `docs/MCP.md` — MCP server tool reference, config snippets for
  popular clients, examples.
- `CLAUDE.md` — agent-facing runbook with hard constraints.
